conventions: Each task is independently implementable once its dependsOn tasks are done. Every code task ends green on `deno task check` and `deno task lint`; tasks with tests also end green on `deno task test`. Sizes: S = well under an hour, M = an hour or two.
release: Ship as 0.0.11. Widening UserMessage.contents is breaking for consumers that assume TextContent[] — acceptable pre-1.0, but call it out in the release notes.

tasks.title: FileContent primitive + fail-loud baseline in all adapters
tasks.description: Add FileContent to src/mod.ts: { file: { mimeType: string; data?: string; url?: string; name?: string } } with JSDoc stating: mimeType is IANA; data is base64 WITHOUT data-URL prefix; exactly one of data/url must be set (enforced at runtime by adapters); name is an optional file name (OpenAI requires one for PDFs). Widen UserMessage.contents to string | (TextContent | FileContent)[]. SystemMessage stays string | TextContent[]. Re-export FileContent from src/model/mod.ts. This widening breaks type-check in every adapter, so the same task updates each one minimally: widen the user-content handling signatures (textFrom in anthropic/openai/mistral, the inline join in ollama, genAIPartsFrom/genAIPartFrom param types in google) and throw RangeError(`<provider> adapter does not support file content yet`) when a file part is encountered on a user message. System-message paths keep their current behavior. No mapping logic yet — that lands per-adapter in media-03..07.
acceptance: FileContent exported from @huuma/ai and @huuma/ai/model
A UserMessage with contents [{ text }, { file: { mimeType, data } }] compiles
deno task check, lint, and test all green (existing tests unchanged)

tasks.title: Shared file-source helpers (src/model/file.ts)
tasks.description: Helpers the adapters share, re-exported from src/model/mod.ts. fileSourceFrom(file: FileContent['file']): { kind: 'data'; data: string } | { kind: 'url'; url: string } — throws RangeError('file content requires exactly one of data or url') when both or neither are set (empty string counts as unset). dataUrlFrom(file): string — returns `data:${mimeType};base64,${data}`, throws RangeError when the file has no data. Keep it to these two; per-provider mimeType routing stays in the adapters.
acceptance: Tests: data-only, url-only, both set throws, neither set throws, empty-string data treated as unset, dataUrlFrom composes the exact data URL and throws on url-only input
deno task test green

tasks.title: Anthropic mapping: image + PDF blocks
tasks.description: In anthropicMessagesFrom, split the system/user branch: system keeps textFrom; user messages whose contents contain a file part send a content block array (TextContent -> { type: 'text', text }, FileContent -> below), otherwise keep today's plain-string path. Mapping via fileSourceFrom: image/* with data -> { type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', media_type, data } } where media_type MUST be one of image/jpeg|png|gif|webp (SDK literal union) — any other image mime throws RangeError; image/* with url -> { type: 'image', source: { type: 'url', url } }; application/pdf with data -> { type: 'document', source: { type: 'base64', media_type: 'application/pdf', data } }; application/pdf with url -> { type: 'document', source: { type: 'url', url } }; any other mimeType throws RangeError naming the provider and mimeType.
acceptance: Tests: image base64, image url, pdf base64, pdf url, image/tiff throws, audio/mpeg throws, text-only user message still sends string content, mixed text+image preserves part order
deno task test green

tasks.title: OpenAI mapping: image_url, input_audio, file part
tasks.description: User messages whose contents contain a file part send content as ChatCompletionContentPart[] (text -> { type: 'text', text }), otherwise keep the plain-string path. Mapping: image/* -> { type: 'image_url', image_url: { url: url ?? dataUrlFrom(file) } }; audio/wav or audio/mpeg with data -> { type: 'input_audio', input_audio: { data, format: 'wav' | 'mp3' } } (audio by url throws — chat completions has no audio URL input); application/pdf with data -> { type: 'file', file: { filename: name ?? 'file.pdf', file_data: dataUrlFrom(file) } } (filename is optional in SDK types but required at wire level); application/pdf with url throws; any other mimeType throws RangeError naming provider and mimeType.
acceptance: Tests: image base64 becomes data URL, image url passes through, audio/wav and audio/mpeg map to formats wav/mp3, audio by url throws, pdf base64 gets data URL + default filename, pdf with explicit name keeps it, pdf by url throws, video/mp4 throws, text-only stays string content
deno task test green

tasks.title: Google mapping: inlineData / fileData part
tasks.description: Add a '"file" in content' branch to genAIPartFrom (before the existing RangeError fallthrough): fileSourceFrom kind 'data' -> { inlineData: { mimeType, data } }; kind 'url' -> { fileData: { fileUri: url, mimeType } }. No mimeType filtering — Gemini accepts images/audio/video/PDF through the same shape; let the API reject unsupported types. Document in a code comment that fileUri is verified for Files-API URIs and YouTube URLs while arbitrary HTTP URLs are doc-ambiguous (CONTEXT.md §2).
acceptance: Tests: data -> inlineData with mimeType, url -> fileData with fileUri, video/mp4 data passes through as inlineData (no filtering), both-set throws via helper
deno task test green

tasks.title: Mistral mapping: image_url, document_url, input_audio chunks
tasks.description: User messages whose contents contain a file part send content as ContentChunk[] (text -> { type: 'text', text }), otherwise keep the plain-string path. Mapping: image/* -> { type: 'image_url', imageUrl: url ?? dataUrlFrom(file) }; application/pdf with url -> { type: 'document_url', documentUrl: url }; application/pdf with data throws (document_url is URL-only — Files API signed-URL flow is out of scope); audio/* -> { type: 'input_audio', inputAudio: data ?? url } (Mistral docs accept base64 or URL in the same string field); any other mimeType throws RangeError naming provider and mimeType.
acceptance: Tests: image base64 becomes data URL, image url passes through, pdf url maps to document_url, pdf base64 throws, audio base64 and audio url both map to input_audio, video/mp4 throws, text-only stays string content
deno task test green

tasks.title: Ollama mapping: images array
tasks.description: In ollamaMessagesFrom's user branch: join text parts into content as today; collect file parts where mimeType is image/* and data is set into the message's images: string[] (base64 passed through verbatim, order preserved). image/* with url throws RangeError (Ollama has no URL source and adapters never fetch); any non-image mimeType throws RangeError naming provider and mimeType.
acceptance: Tests: single image base64 lands in images[], two images preserve order alongside joined text content, image by url throws, application/pdf throws, text-only message has no images field
deno task test green

tasks.title: Widen Agent.run prompt to accept file parts
tasks.description: Change run(prompt: string, ...) to run(prompt: string | (TextContent | FileContent)[], ...). The prompt flows unchanged into { role: 'user', contents: prompt } (src/agent/mod.ts:172), so this is a type-level widening plus JSDoc: document that media support depends on the model adapter and provider, and unsupported combinations throw at request time. Update the @param docs.
acceptance: Test: run() with [{ text }, { file: { mimeType: 'image/png', data } }] emits a user message whose contents carry both parts verbatim (existing fake-model test pattern)
deno task test green

tasks.title: ADR 0003: media as a mimeType-keyed file content part
tasks.description: Record the decisions from CONTEXT.md §4–5 in the style of ADR 0001/0002: one property-discriminated FileContent part keyed by IANA mimeType (not per-category parts); raw base64 + mimeType as canonical encoding (not data URLs); exactly-one-of data/url enforced at runtime by adapters; fail-loud RangeError on provider-unsupported combinations (no silent dropping, no auto-fetching URLs in adapters); phase 2 (media in tool results) and phase 3 (model-output media) deferred with the forward-compatibility argument. Include the rejected alternatives with their reasons.
acceptance: ADR follows the existing docs/adr structure (context, decision, consequences, rejected alternatives)

tasks.title: README + module docs for media input
tasks.description: README: a short 'Media input' section with one runnable example (agent().run with an image FileContent), the provider capability summary from CONTEXT.md §2 as a compact table (image/PDF/audio/URL per provider), and the fail-loud policy in one sentence. src/mod.ts module JSDoc: add a FileContent usage example next to the existing Message example.
acceptance: README example type-checks when pasted into a scratch file (deno check against the local package)
deno task lint green
